symptoms.symptom_name: Gradual painless bilateral blur with disabling glare and poor night vision

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Painless progressive blur and glare with light-scatter are the classic cataract picture.

symptoms.symptom_name: Insidious painless loss of peripheral field (tunnel vision)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Painless progressive peripheral field loss with preserved central vision is characteristic of open-angle glaucoma.
Absence of acute pain, red eye, halos and a fixed mid-dilated pupil argues against the acute closed-angle form.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden loss of all functional vision when ambient light failed, with disorientation, then rapid recovery on restored light

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: environment-triggered fall + situational panic on chronic low vision
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A clear mechanical trigger with rapid reversal on restored light points to an environmental, not neurological, cause.
Transient bilateral visual failure keyed to the blackout and reversing with light is not a fixed focal deficit.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unaccustomed trip-and-fall over a rug in the dark

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: occult fracture or head injury from the fall
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Any fall in a frail older adult carries occult-injury risk that requires in-person examination to exclude.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute anxiety with palpitations and fear of stroke, settling with reassurance and restored light

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A self-limiting panic response provoked by sudden sensory deprivation, resolving as the trigger is removed.

symptoms.symptom_name: No loss of consciousness, no head strike, no new focal weakness or speech disturbance

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of focal deficit and LOC lowers, but from telehealth cannot fully exclude, an acute neurological event.

symptoms.symptom_name: No bony point tenderness, full range of motion, grossly intact cranial nerves; penlight lens opacification and confrontation tunnel vision

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: occult fracture or head injury from the fall
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician in-person examination cleared fracture and gross neurology — a sealed measurement the AI Doctor cannot obtain and must not assume.

symptoms.symptom_name: Blood pressure 155/90, heart rate 95, SpO2 98%, RR 20, temp 36.6

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mildly elevated BP and HR consistent with acute distress; a sealed clinician measurement the AI cannot obtain remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Elevated intraocular pressure / optic-disc cupping on specialist ophthalmic examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Anticipated specialist findings that confirm chronic glaucoma; never available in real time via telehealth.
